headline: Inflation may have peaked in May, per Kalshi traders
detail: If June CPI prints soft, rate-cut odds move forward and long-duration tech/growth names rally. This is the single biggest macro tailwind for your QQQ, SMH, CRWD, and SOXL positions. However, this is market expectation, not confirmed data - a surprise to the upside reverses the move hard.
rec: No new buys yet - wait for the actual print. A soft CPI would be the moment to consider adding SMH near current levels given its -1.59% drag; scanner has no SMH setup today so no mechanical entry to anchor to.

headline: CoreWeave, Nebius tumble on Meta cloud threat
detail: Meta positioning as a serious cloud competitor pressures pure-play AI infrastructure names. You don't hold CoreWeave or Nebius, but CRWD and broader tech sentiment can get dragged in risk-off AI rotations. Worth watching whether this spreads to semiconductor demand narratives.
rec: No action on your holdings today from this headline alone - CRWD is cybersecurity, not cloud infrastructure, so direct impact is low. Just track whether the selloff broadens into semis, which would pressure SOXL further.

headline: Nvidia betting on trillion-dollar robotics boom
detail: Robotics is the next AI hardware cycle and Nvidia sits at the center - this keeps the long-term bull case for semis intact. SOXL and SMH both have indirect exposure to wherever Nvidia leads chip demand. But 'hidden way to trade it' framing suggests this is a thematic idea, not an immediate catalyst.
rec: No trade today - this is a longer-term thesis, not a same-day catalyst. If you want robotics exposure, that's a separate position decision, not something to layer onto existing leveraged semi risk you're already managing.

headline: Palantir's Karp bashes OpenAI and Anthropic token model
detail: Karp's comments signal fractures in how AI companies monetize - if the token-based model is questioned, it raises uncertainty around AI software spending cycles. You don't hold Palantir directly, but this kind of AI narrative noise can create short-term volatility across your tech-heavy book.
rec: Nothing to act on today - this is competitive positioning commentary, not a fundamental shift. Keep your stops in place and don't let sentiment headlines chase you out of solid setups like CRWD.
